title: VXLAN Tunnel and Overlay Health
dataSources: VXLAN tunnel status, NVE interface, encapsulation stats
spl:
index=sdn sourcetype="vxlan:tunnel"
| where state!="up" OR packet_drops > 0
| stats latest(state) as state, sum(packet_drops) as drops by tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni
| table tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni, state, drops
description: VXLAN tunnel failures break overlay connectivity. Monitoring tunnel state and packet drops ensures fabric reliability and fast troubleshooting.
value: VXLAN tunnel failures break overlay connectivity. Monitoring tunnel state and packet drops ensures fabric reliability and fast troubleshooting.
implementation: Poll VXLAN tunnel and NVE stats from fabric devices. Alert on tunnel down or non-zero drops. Report on overlay health by VNI and leaf. Correlate with BGP and underlay events.
visualization: Status grid (tunnel × state), Table (tunnels with drops), Line chart (drops over time).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Leaf/spine device logs, fabric manager.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: VXLAN tunnel status, NVE interface, encapsulation stats.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll VXLAN tunnel and NVE stats from fabric devices. Alert on tunnel down or non-zero drops. Report on overlay health by VNI and leaf. Correlate with BGP and underlay events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sdn sourcetype="vxlan:tunnel"
| where state!="up" OR packet_drops > 0
| stats latest(state) as state, sum(packet_drops) as drops by tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni
| table tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni, state, drops
```

Understanding this SPL

**VXLAN Tunnel and Overlay Health** — VXLAN tunnel failures break overlay connectivity. Monitoring tunnel state and packet drops ensures fabric reliability and fast troubleshooting.

Documented **Data sources**: VXLAN tunnel status, NVE interface, encapsulation stats. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Leaf/spine device logs, fabric manager. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sdn; **sourcetype**: vxlan:tunnel. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sdn, sourcetype="vxlan:tunnel". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where state!="up" OR packet_drops > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **VXLAN Tunnel and Overlay Health**): table tunnel_id, leaf_id, vni, state, drops


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (tunnel × state), Table (tunnels with drops), Line chart (drops over time).
grandmaExplanation: VXLAN tunnel failures break overlay connectivity — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
